description_long: This is a military-style, twin-engine propeller airplane with a camouflage paint scheme, featuring dark green, olive, and yellow tones. The wings are straight and mounted at a low position on the fuselage, each supporting a large engine nacelle with visible propellers. The fuselage is streamlined, with a glass-enclosed cockpit situated at the front, and the tail section includes a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers. Distinctive markings, including crosses on the wings and fuselage, suggest a historical or World War II-era design. The landing gear is not visible, indicating it may be retractable. The overall construction appears to be primarily metal, with glass for the cockpit canopy and some rubber components for tires and seals.
description: A twin-propeller, low-wing military airplane with camouflage paint, glass cockpit, and historical markings. Features a streamlined fuselage and single vertical tail fin.
description_view_0: Front view showing the twin propellers, glass cockpit, and wide wingspan.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the camouflage pattern, wing markings, and engine nacelles.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail section, single vertical stabilizer, and horizontal stabilizers.
description_view_3: Side view showing the streamlined fuselage, cockpit placement, and engine nacelle profile.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, straight wings with no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Twin piston engines mounted on the wings with visible propellers.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Landing gear not visible; likely retractable based on design.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a multi-panel glass canopy.
